title: Weekly Client Health Summary
prompt: Generate a weekly client health summary for our agency portfolio. For each client, include: overall health score (0-100), commit activity trend (up/down/stable), open PR count with aging analysis (flag any >7 days), open issue count, last deploy date, and any risk alerts. Format as a concise executive brief with color-coded status (green/yellow/red) and recommended actions for any client scoring below 70.

title: Stale PR Triage & Action Plan
prompt: Review the following list of open pull requests for [CLIENT_NAME]. For each PR older than 7 days, analyze: 1) Is this blocked by review, CI, or merge conflicts? 2) What is the risk of merging vs. closing? 3) Suggest a priority order for addressing them. Group PRs into: merge immediately, needs review, needs author update, consider closing. Include a draft Slack message to the team summarizing the triage results.

title: Deploy Readiness Check
prompt: Perform a deploy readiness assessment for [CLIENT_NAME]'s [REPO_NAME] repository. Check: 1) Are all CI checks passing on main? 2) Are there any open PRs that should be merged first? 3) What changed since the last deploy? 4) Any dependency updates pending? 5) Are there migration scripts that need to run? Output a go/no-go recommendation with a checklist of pre-deploy steps.

title: Client Risk Alert Draft
prompt: Draft an internal risk alert for [CLIENT_NAME] whose health score has dropped to [SCORE]. Include: 1) What metrics triggered the alert (stale PRs, no commits, failing CI, etc.) 2) Root cause analysis — is this a staffing issue, scope creep, technical debt, or client responsiveness? 3) Recommended immediate actions (next 48 hours) 4) Medium-term remediation plan (next 2 weeks). Tone should be factual and solution-oriented, not alarmist.

title: Repository Activity Deep Dive
prompt: Analyze the commit and PR activity for [CLIENT_NAME] over the past 30 days. Break down: 1) Commits per week trend 2) Average PR review time 3) PR merge rate vs. close rate 4) Top contributors 5) Code areas with most churn. Identify any patterns — is velocity increasing or decreasing? Are there bottlenecks in the review process? Recommend optimizations to improve delivery health.
